content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be reengineered to dynamically weight the retention of episodic memory traces based on the intrinsic curiosity signal generated by ICM/RND curiosity-driven reinforcement learning methods, eliminating the persistent problem of low-relevance memory bloat that plagues both current SOTA active agent memory and open-ended curious agents. This hybrid design creates a self-reinforcing cycle where the agent only prioritizes storing novel, information-dense experiences that will improve future active learning sample efficiency, rather than clogging memory with predictable, low-value observations.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
